steps.title: Read set A from the dot plot
steps.content: The dot plot for set A gives a mean of $8\,\mu$F and a range (largest minus smallest) of $12\,\mu$F.

steps.title: Use the shift rule
steps.content: Set B is formed by adding the constant $17\,\mu$F to every value in set A. Adding a constant shifts the mean by that constant and leaves the range unchanged.

steps.title: Compute set B
steps.content: Mean of B $= 8 + 17 = 25\,\mu$F; range of B $= 12\,\mu$F.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A (mean $8$, range $12$): forgets to add $17$ to the mean.
  • Choice B (mean $8$, range $29$): wrong mean, and a shift cannot change the range.
  • Choice C (mean $25$, range $12$): correct mean and correct unchanged range.
  • Choice D (mean $25$, range $29$): right mean but range incorrectly increased by $17$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
